# Beudox HR Management System - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based HR management system built with TypeScript, using Supabase as the backend-as-a-service platform. The application follows a component-driven architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Frontend Architecture
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript for type safety
- **Build Tool**: Vite with SWC for fast compilation
- **Routing**: React Router v6 with protected routes and role-based access control
- **State Management**: React Query for server state, React hooks for local state
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens and shadcn/ui component system
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation

### Backend Architecture
- **Platform**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Real-time + Edge Functions)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with automated migrations
- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with email/password and magic links
- **API**: Supabase client for database operations and real-time subscriptions
- **Server Functions**: Supabase Edge Functions for complex business logic

## Key Files and Structure

### Core Application Files
- `src/main.tsx` - Application entry point, renders App component
- `src/App.tsx` - Main application component with routing configuration
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx` - Main layout wrapper with sidebar and navigation

### Authentication & Authorization
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx` - Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts` - Role-based access control utilities
- Protected routes implemented in `App.tsx` with `canAccess()` checks

### Data Layer
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` - Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` - Auto-generated TypeScript types from database schema
- React Query hooks throughout the app for data fetching and mutations

### Component Organization
- `src/components/ui/` - Reusable UI components (shadcn/ui)
- `src/components/layout/` - Layout-specific components
- Feature-specific components organized by domain (employee-profile, settings, etc.)

### Utility Libraries
- `src/lib/utils.ts` - General utilities (cn function for class merging)
- `src/lib/format-date.ts` - Date formatting utilities
- `src/lib/role-access.ts` - Permission checking functions
- `src/lib/notifications.ts` - Notification system utilities

## Database Schema Highlights

The application manages comprehensive HR data including:
- **Employees**: Profiles, roles, departments, contact information
- **Attendance**: Daily check-in/check-out records, overtime tracking
- **Leave**: Leave requests, balances, approval workflows
- **Payroll**: Salary structures, deductions, payslip generation
- **Evaluations**: Performance reviews, evaluation parameters
- **Projects**: Project management, team assignments, activity logs
- **Finance**: Invoices, expenses, financial reporting

## Business Logic Patterns

### Attendance Tracking
- Automatic overtime calculation (regular + holiday OT)
- Working hours computation from check-in/check-out times
- Status determination (Present, Absent, Late, Holiday, Weekend)

### Leave Management
- Balance tracking with automatic updates
- Working days calculation excluding weekends/holidays
- Approval workflow with status tracking

### Payroll Processing
- Automated payroll generation via Supabase Edge Functions
- Complex calculations for allowances, deductions, arrears
- PDF generation for payslips and invoices

### Role-Based Access
- Route-level protection in App.tsx
- Component-level permission checks
- Hierarchical role system with granular permissions

## Development Patterns

### Component Patterns
- Functional components with TypeScript interfaces
- Custom hooks for shared logic
- Consistent prop interfaces with optional parameters
- Error boundaries and loading states

### Data Fetching
- React Query for caching and synchronization
- Optimistic updates for better UX
- Error handling with user-friendly messages
- Real-time subscriptions for live data

### Form Handling
- React Hook Form for complex forms
- Zod schemas for validation
- Controlled components with proper TypeScript typing
- File upload handling with drag-and-drop

## Configuration and Environment

### Build Configuration
- `vite.config.ts` - Vite configuration with path aliases and optimization
- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts` - Tailwind CSS configuration with custom theme

### Environment Variables
- Supabase project configuration
- Build-time environment detection
- Development vs production settings

## Testing Strategy

- **Unit Tests**: Vitest for component and utility testing
- **Integration Tests**: Playwright for end-to-end testing
- **Component Tests**: React Testing Library for component behavior

## Performance Considerations

- Code splitting with Vite
- React Query caching and background refetching
- Optimized bundle with tree shaking
- Lazy loading for routes and heavy components
- Database indexing via Supabase migrations

## Security Measures

- Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) policies
- Authentication required for all protected routes
- Role-based access control throughout the application
- Input validation with Zod schemas
- SQL injection prevention via Supabase client

## Deployment

- Built as a static SPA deployable to any static hosting
- Supabase handles backend scaling and maintenance
- Environment-specific configuration
- Automated build and deployment pipelines
